title: KDB Compiled Source Response (per-call model output)
description: Exactly one source -> exactly one response object. The LLM emits this shape per compile call. Run-level fields (run_id, success, aggregate errors) are Python-owned and NOT present here; see compile_result.schema.json. This contract is stricter than compile_result.schema.json's pageIntent (all 8 page fields required) so the model must commit to status, support, links, and confidence on every page rather than relying on Python backfill.

slug.description: Lowercase ASCII kebab-case identifier. No paths, no .md suffixes, no directory separators.

sourceId.description: Canonical relative path of a raw source. The LLM echoes the id it was given verbatim.

pageIntent.description: One page the LLM wants to create or replace (full-body model, D18). Stricter than compile_result.schema.json: all 8 fields required, no Python backfill.

body.description: Full markdown body, no frontmatter. Python prepends frontmatter during apply.

supports_page_existence.description: source_ids that justify this page existing. Every page MUST include the compile call's source_id.

outgoing_links.description: Slugs this page's body links to. Must appear in body as [[slug]]. Python reconciles incoming_links_known from these.
